Title: Evolution of L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity allows for recurrent specialisation to betalain pigmentation in Caryophyllales

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: DODAa1 orthologues (e.g., BvDODAa1; ShDODAa1; McDODAa1; CgDODAa1)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Beta vulgaris; Stegnosperma halimifolium; Mesembryanthemum crystallinum; Carnegiea gigantea
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Beta vulgaris; Stegnosperma halimifolium; Mesembryanthemum crystallinum; Carnegiea gigantea
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Beta vulgaris subsp. vulgaris 'Bolivar'/'YTiBv' for beet material; others NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome sequencing for Simmondsia chinensis and Kewa bowkeriana (accession NA); genome sequencing for Corrigiola litoralis, Limeum aethiopicum, Simmondsia chinensis and Spergula arvensis (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong betanin/betalain accumulation in heterologous assay, indicating high L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity and betalamic acid production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase reaction converting L-DOPA to betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression/agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana with multigene betalain constructs; HPLC betanin quantification; comparative functional assay across orthologues
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: betalain biosynthesis core step leading to betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High-activity DODAa1 enzymes are polyphyletic and represent recurrent specialization after gene duplication; activity levels vary among orthologues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Betalain (measured as betanin; immediate enzymatic product betalamic acid)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf agroinfiltration; 4 d post-infiltration sampling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf infiltration spots in Nicotiana benthamiana assay; naturally associated with pigmented tissues in Caryophyllales
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for engineering betalain pigmentation; high-activity orthologues can be selected for metabolic engineering, but lineage-specific specialization should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only one DODAa paralogue in each betalain-pigmented species showed high L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity. 2. These high-activity orthologues strongly increased betanin accumulation in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. High activity evolved recurrently and polyphyletically after DODAa duplication.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: DODAa2 and other low-activity DODAa paralogues (e.g., McDODAa2; BvDODAa2-5; CgDODAa2; MaDODAa; KcDODAa1; KcDODAa2; CrDODAa; TiDODAa)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Mesembryanthemum crystallinum; Beta vulgaris; Carnegiea gigantea; Macarthuria australis; Kewa caespitosa; Cardionema ramosissimum; Telephium imperati
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome and genome resources as above; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces marginal or no betanin accumulation, indicating low or negligible L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Weakly catalyzes or fails to catalyze conversion of L-DOPA to betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression/agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marginal activity is widespread in both betalain- and anthocyanin-pigmented taxa and suggests catalytic promiscuity; primary native function remains unknown
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf infiltration spots in Nicotiana benthamiana assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less suitable for engineering than DODAa1; may provide insight into evolutionary intermediates and enzyme optimization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Numerous DODAa paralogues across betalainic and anthocyanic species showed only marginal activity. 2. These enzymes generated low betanin levels in the heterologous assay. 3. Widespread low activity supports a promiscuous ancestral background from which high activity evolved recurrently.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Stegnosperma halimifolium; Beta vulgaris; Mesembryanthemum crystallinum; Carnegiea gigantea
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows no visible betalain pigmentation and only trace betanin, indicating negligible L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: does not substantially contribute to target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: No meaningful L-DOPA to betalamic acid conversion detected in planta proxy assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression/agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: related paralog outside specialized high-activity branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: DODA family paralogue outside betalain-specialized DODAa high-activity branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports conclusion that high L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity evolved exclusively within DODAa lineage, not DODAb
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf infiltration spots in Nicotiana benthamiana assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Poor candidate for betalain engineering; useful as evolutionary comparison showing non-specialized paralogue state
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DODAb genes from betalain-pigmented species did not produce visible betacyanin in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. HPLC detected only trace betanin (<0.1% of BvDODAa1). 3. High L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity is restricted to the DODAa lineage.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Beta vulgaris; Mesembryanthemum crystallinum; Chenopodium quinoa; Amaranthus hypochondriacus
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resources used for synteny analysis; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required partner gene in multigene construct enabling betalain production; genomic colocalization with DODAa1 is associated with betalain cluster in Amaranthaceae
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Tyrosine hydroxylase activity leading to L-DOPA formation for downstream betalamic acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Used in transient betalain production constructs; comparative synteny analysis of genomic colocalization with DODAa1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the primary focus of functional assays in this paper, but central pathway partner discussed in relation to gene clustering and precursor evolution
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf agroinfiltration for construct support; natural genomic context for synteny
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking partner with DODAa1 for betalain engineering; cluster context may aid coordinated expression in Amaranthaceae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AD1 was included as a necessary structural gene in the heterologous betalain pathway construct. 2. DODAa1 and CYP76AD1 are colocalized in Amaranthaceae genomes but not in Mesembryanthemum crystallinum. 3. The paper proposes CYP76AD duplication/tyrosine hydroxylase activity as an evolutionary precursor to betalain biosynthesis.
